topic: Solving and Graphing Linear Equations and Inequalities

misconceptions.title: Wrong Slope in Graphs
misconceptions.description: Mistakes slope by directly mapping coefficients without converting to slope-intercept. Results in an incorrect boundary line on inequality graphs.

misconceptions.title: Inclusive vs. Exclusive Boundary Error
misconceptions.description: Fails to distinguish between inclusive and exclusive inequalities. Uses solid lines incorrectly for strict inequalities.
successIndicators: Choose dashed lines for strict inequalities

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Sign Reversal in Equations
misconceptions.description: Mistakenly maintains incorrect sign while switching x and y or while solving inequalities, leading to wrong solution.

misconceptions.title: Failing to Solve After Substitution
misconceptions.description: Substitutes correctly for one variable but fails to find the complete solution by not substituting back to find other variables.
